query_normalized: evaluating the impact of data scaling on statistical test sensitivity statistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) because it explicitly investigates the performance of statistical tests (specifically "unified consistent tests") across different data scaling regimes ("sparse, dense and ultra-dense cases"), which directly measures the sensitivity of statistical inference as a function of data volume/structure.
